ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-15-2010 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-01-2010 Continued To Sep-15-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-17-2010 Continued To Sep-01-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Rowena Navia for Kathy Nakaqawa.

Ms. Navia stated that she filed the settlement agreement and mutual release, asked the Court to allow filing of an ex parte petition for approval of the settlement agreement, requested that the hearing on the accounting be scheduled for September 1st or September 8th and stated that there were no objections. The Court will allow Counsel to file the ex parte petition for approval of the settlement agreement. The accounting was continued to Septmeber 1, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-22-2010 Continued To Aug-17-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Rowena Navia for and with Kathy Nakagawa. Eric Steinhauer for and with Morgan Carter (Objector).

Mr. Steinhauer stated that some discovery would be needed, that there was a two year delay by Ms. Nakagawa, that Ms. Nakagawa should have filed a status report, that the statements and expenses were being disputed, that he was not served with the supplemental declaration, spoke of a surcharge issue and stated that Morgan's needs had not been met for two years. Ms. Navia explained that the supplemental declaration was prepared to address the Examiner's notes, apologized for not sending to Counsel, stated that the Estate was preserved and protected, that there was difficulty with communicating with the Father and that Ms. Nakagawa looked for someone to serve as Trustee. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation (order for the same to be signed). Mediation is to be completed by August 10, 2010. The matter was continued to August 17, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.
